name: Reine De Saba
recipeInstructions:
1. For the nicest looking shiny glaze, serve this cake on the day it is completed. The cake remains moist and delicious for several days.
2. Preheat oven to 325°F. Butter and flour an 8-inch square or 10-inch round cake pan.
3. Cut the chocolate into small pieces and melt in the top of a double boiler set over hot water, stirring frequently. Remove from hot water and cool slightly.
4. In a food processor, nut grinder, or blender, grind the almonds to a powder. If using a processor or blender, put 2 tablespoons of sugar with the nuts. Mix until no chunks remain.
5. Toss the powdered almond mixture with the flour.
6. Separate the eggs. Beat together butter and 1/3 cup of sugar until creamy and light. Add melted chocolate and extract. Add egg yolks one at a time, beating thoroughly after each addition.
7. Beat egg whites to stiff peaks, add remaining sugar, and beat until glossy. Fold in nut mixture gently.
8. Gently fold the egg white mixture into the chocolate just until incorporated. Pour batter into prepared pan.
9. Bake in preheated oven until a toothpick inserted in center comes out almost clean (about 30 minutes). Cool on a rack for about 10 minutes.
10. Divide into individual servings if desired. This recipe can be made up to this point up to a day ahead.
11. For the chocolate glaze, heat chocolate and butter in top of double boiler set over hot water, stirring frequently until just melted. Remove from hot water and cool slightly. It should remain pourable but thick enough to coat the cake.
12. Drizzle glaze over cooled cake or individual servings on a rack. Garnish with almonds if desired.
13. Serve at room temperature or refrigerate for up to 4 days.
